title: BackgroundService 'fire-and-forget' comment contradicts await
description: In BackgroundService.start(), the comment says 'Fire-and-forget: service starts regardless of grant or deny.' But the code immediately below does 'await _requestNotificationPermission()'. The method blocks until the permission dialog resolves (granted or denied), then continues to startService. 'Fire-and-forget' means non-blocking, which this is not. A contributor reading this will assume the permission and service start are decoupled and may refactor to actually make it fire-and-forget, breaking the sequencing guarantee. Fix: rewrite comment to 'Await the permission dialog so the notification system dialog appears before startService; service starts regardless of outcome.'
